title: CAPM: If the expected rate of return of a stock is...
summary: Apply the Capital Asset Pricing Model to calculate expected returns or evaluate security pricing.
prompt: If the expected rate of return of a stock is 12% and its beta is 1.2, what would be the required return based on the CAPM if the risk-free rate is 3%?
solution: CAPM formula: E(r) = r_f + β[E(r_m) - r_f]. Here, E(r) = 3% + 1.2(12% - 3%) = 12%.
